name: ts-error
usage_summary: Re-exported from packages/cli-kit/src/public/node/error.ts as `export {ExtendableError} from 'ts-error'`. Consumed in four places: cli-kit/session/exchange.ts (two subclasses), cli-kit/plugins/tunnel.ts (one subclass), cli-kit/result.ts (instanceof check), and packages/app/src/cli/services/dev/extension/localization.ts (throws an instance).
how_to_remove: ts-error's ExtendableError is a single-class shim that fixes the `instanceof` chain for Error subclasses in ES5 targets. Modern TypeScript with ES2015+ targets handles this natively. Replace the re-export in error.ts with a locally defined class: `export class ExtendableError extends Error { constructor(message?: string) { super(message); this.name = new.target.name; } }`. No downstream call sites need to change.
replacement_reason: The entire package provides one class (~10 lines) that is now unnecessary with ES2015+ targets. Inlining eliminates the dependency with a trivial one-time change in a single file.
